text: Mobile app development has become increasingly accessible, but choosing the right approach is crucial. Developers must decide between native and cross-platform development. Native apps are built specifically for one platform, like iOS or Android, offering better performance and access to device features. In contrast, cross-platform tools allow a single codebase to run on multiple systems, saving time and money. Regardless of the method, responsive design is essential to ensure the app looks good on different screen sizes. Once developed, apps are submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store, where they must meet quality guidelines. Performance is also critical—users expect fast loading times and smooth interactions, or they will quickly abandon the app.
